visual_qa_issues_found: Due Diligence sections were too equal-weight after the parcel header.
The review flow needed a concise high-priority triage layer before detailed domain sections.
Two-column fact grids in the side panel risked cramped long labels and values.
Some empty values used less polished unavailable wording.
Executive Print needed the same priority-review readout on the first report page.
Print CSS needed stronger overflow guards and coverage for the new card background classes.
due_diligence_polish_changes: Added 2. High-Priority Review Flags after Parcel Snapshot.
Renumbered the detailed review flow through 11. Review Notes / Recommended Next Steps.
Converted selected-parcel fact grids to single-column side-panel layout for better scanability.
Improved card hierarchy with slightly stronger card backgrounds, icon wells, and section shadows.
Updated status badges to wrap safely instead of clipping.
Standardized empty values toward Not Available, Official Data Needed, Capacity Data Needed, Proxy Only, Current Context, and Internal Research Only language.
Kept all required flood, school, transportation, utility, zoning, permit, and model caveats.
executive_print_polish_changes: Added High-Priority Review Flags to the printable report before detailed domain sections.
Improved report section hierarchy with stronger section cards and safer badge wrapping.
Kept report structure as a planning memo rather than a dashboard screenshot.
Preserved report header, generated timestamp, selected parcel ID, key findings, limitations, and recommended actions.
print_css_changes: Added print overflow guards to prevent clipped long labels or values.
Added print background overrides for the new Due Diligence and Executive Print card classes.
Kept interactive controls, map UI, sidebars, and buttons hidden in print.
Preserved light/white print theme and page-break helpers.

prediction_safety_verification.model_status_language: Internal research only; parcel-level model outputs are not exposed.

browser_smoke_results.parcel_search: passed using direct keypress input because browser fill helper reported a virtual clipboard issue

remaining_known_issues: Actual browser print preview was not programmatically inspected; CSS was hardened for print and report DOM was smoke-tested.
Existing ArcGIS non-fatal warnings may still appear for draped label placement or placeholder layer definitions.
Transportation and utility sections remain current-context/proxy summaries only until parcel-level authoritative capacity/access data is added.
